name: openmind-code-graphs
description: Open Mind's deterministic code-graphs skill, run through the Open Mind skill bridge. Given 'where is <symbol> defined' or 'who uses <symbol>', answer from the structure map recovered from the corpus's real code (definition index + name-based call/usage graph). No invented nodes or edges; unknown symbols return insufficient_evidence.

input.description: A structure question of the form 'where is <symbol> defined' or 'who uses <symbol>'.

fields.name: question
fields.description: The request, e.g. 'where is post_entry defined'.

output.description: Structured JSON with a status, an answer, claims citing real definition sites, and the tool calls used.

tools.name: symbol_definition
tools.description: Definition site(s) of a symbol from Open Mind's deterministic structure map (file:line:kind).

tools.name: symbol_usage
tools.description: Files referencing a symbol, from Open Mind's name-based call/usage graph. Only used for 'who uses' questions.

citationRequirement: Every claim must cite {file, line} inside the fixture corpus; definition claims must cite the actual definition line (which carries the symbol).
unsupportedClaimPolicy: Graph facts are recovered from the code — defs, imports, call sites — never generated. A symbol absent from the corpus must return insufficient_evidence with zero claims.
failureBehavior: Unknown symbols return status=insufficient_evidence with an empty claims array. Never invent a definition site, a caller, or an edge.
validationRules: Output must match the required JSON schema.
Every citation must reference an existing corpus file and an in-range line number.
Each cited line must support its claim (shared keyword).
When status is answered, the queried symbol must appear on a cited line and the expected file must be cited.
Forbidden claims (files the symbol is NOT used in) must not appear in the output.
symbol_definition must be called, and before symbol_usage when both are used.
